ground_truth.description: Photo of an old and small church/chapel in Skeikampen, Norway.
ground_truth.photographer_description: Photo of an old and small church/chapel in Skeikampen, Norway. I expect it to recognize that it is a church/chapel and should maybe be able to guess the country. Beyond that, I'm not expecting it to get the specific one. Daytime, autumn.
key_visual_elements: Wooden cross at the apex (roof peak)
ground_truth.difficulty: Hard (should recognize church, maybe guess country)
ground_truth.notes: Not expecting specific identification. Daytime, autumn.

details.description: Frontal view of a small Nordic mountain church set among coniferous trees. The façade is symmetrical with a steep gabled roof and a small wooden bellcote at the ridge. The nave walls are white-rendered masonry, while a projecting entrance porch is clad in vertical timber boards with a pair of heavy wooden double doors featuring chevron patterns. A tiny square window sits high on the gable. Roofing appears dark, likely shingle or slate. The building stands on a grassy clearing under an overcast sky, giving a quiet alpine-forest setting. The overall look is simple Norwegian vernacular ecclesiastical architecture combining timber and plaster.

details.description: A small chapel-like building stands in a rural setting. The structure has white plastered side walls with a prominent, steep gabled wooden façade at the front. The lower front is clad in dark, weathered wooden boards forming a triangular pediment that frames a large, carved double wooden door with geometric patterns. Above the door, the white wall continues to a small window near the peak. Atop the roof sits a compact wooden belfry with a simple, pitched roof. The surrounding area is grassy with a gravel path leading to the entrance, and tall conifer trees frame the building in the background. The lighting is soft and diffuse, suggesting an overcast sky and a calm, quiet moment in a Nordic landscape.

entities.name: Skei Fjellkirke
entities.notes: Mountain church near Skeikampen; exterior features white plaster walls with a wooden front façade and a small wooden bell tower.

details.description: Centered frontal view of a small Nordic mountain chapel set among conifer trees. The building has white-rendered walls with a steep gabled roof and a small wooden bellcote at the ridge. A timber-clad vestibule with dark vertical boards projects from the front and frames double wooden doors with a chevron pattern. A tiny square window sits high on the main gable. The foreground is a grassy, slightly gravelly clearing under an overcast sky.

details.description: A small rural church in Norway. The building has white plastered walls with a wooden front facade formed by vertical timber planks. A steep gabled roof meets a tiny wooden belfry at the apex. A pair of ornate wooden doors with a chevron/diamond pattern sits at the center of the wooden frontage. Surrounded by evergreen trees and a grassy foreground under an overcast sky.

entities.name: Small rural church
entities.notes: White plastered exterior with wooden front; small wooden belfry atop the roof.

details.description: A symmetrical, eye-level shot of Skei Fjellkirke (Skei Mountain Church) in Norway. The building features a steep gabled roof and a facade composed of two distinct sections: a main white masonry wall behind a protruding, dark wooden entrance structure. The wooden section has vertical timber siding and double doors adorned with a geometric diamond relief pattern. Above the wooden entrance, on the white facade, is a small, solitary square window. At the very apex of the roof sits a small, dark wooden bellcote or turret. The church is flanked by tall spruce trees on either side and sits on a grassy patch with a gravel path leading to the door, under an overcast white sky.

entities.name: Skei Fjellkirke
entities.notes: Also known as Skei Mountain Church; consecrated in 2001; designed by architect Einar Dahle.

details.description: A small, modern mountain church featuring a prominent white-plastered gable end and a steeply pitched roof. A smaller, dark-stained wooden gable structure serves as the entrance porch, housing double wooden doors with a decorative diamond-patterned relief. A tiny square window is positioned high on the white facade. Atop the main roof ridge sits a small, open wooden belfry. The building is situated on a grassy clearing surrounded by evergreen spruce trees under an overcast, white sky. The architecture combines traditional Norwegian materials like wood and stone with a minimalist, contemporary aesthetic.

entities.name: Skei Fjellkirke
entities.notes: A mountain church consecrated in 2001, designed by architect Peter Helland-Hansen.

entities.name: Skeikampen
entities.notes: The mountain and resort area where the church is located.

entities.name: Modern Norwegian Church Architecture
entities.notes: Characterized by minimalist forms and traditional materials like wood and stone.

details.description: Frontal view of a small rural chapel/church. The building has a steep gabled roof, whitewashed/rendered masonry walls, and a projecting timber-clad entrance porch with vertical boards and double wooden doors featuring a chevron pattern. Above the main gable is a tiny square window and a simple timber bellcote/belfry. The structure sits in a grassy clearing bordered by coniferous trees under an overcast sky. The overall look is modest and vernacular, typical of Nordic or Alpine rural chapels.

details.description: A small rural chapel or church with white plastered walls and a prominent, dark wooden, triangular façade extension at the front. The front is clad in vertical wooden planks and features a large, ornate wooden double door with geometric paneling. A tiny square window sits below the roof peak, and a small wooden belfry or bell tower sits perched on the apex of the roof. The building sits in a grassy clearing, bordered by tall evergreen trees, under a pale, overcast sky, creating a stark, minimalist landscape.

details.description: Frontal, centered view of a small rural chapel in a forest clearing. The building has whitewashed stucco walls, a steep gabled roof, and a timber-clad entrance porch projecting forward with a pair of chevron-patterned wooden doors. A tiny square window sits high on the main gable, and a simple gabled bellcote (roof belfry) crowns the ridge. Conifer trees surround the site and the sky is overcast, creating a quiet, rustic atmosphere.

details.description: A small rural chapel with white plaster walls and a prominent, dark wooden front facade. The building features a steep, gabled roof and a small square window near the peak. A wooden belfry-like structure sits atop the roof. The entrance consists of large, carved wooden double doors set into a vertical wooden plank facade. The church is surrounded by a grassy foreground and a backdrop of tall evergreen trees, under an pale, overcast sky that gives the scene a quiet, rustic atmosphere.

details.description: A symmetrical, eye-level shot of the front facade of Storvallens fjällkapell (Storvallen Mountain Chapel). The chapel features a minimalist Nordic design with a steeply pitched gable roof topped by a small, dark wooden bell turret. The main body of the building is finished in white plaster, contrasting sharply with the prominent entrance porch (vestibule) projecting from the center. The porch is clad in dark, vertical wooden planks and has its own steep gable roof, echoing the main structure. The entrance features double wooden doors adorned with a geometric diamond pattern. A single, small square window is positioned high on the white facade above the porch. The building is surrounded by tall evergreen spruce trees, typical of the Swedish mountain region, under an overcast, white sky. The ground in the foreground is covered in grass.

entities.notes: Also known as Storvallen Mountain Chapel; located near Storlien.

details.description: A front-facing view of a minimalist chapel featuring a stark white plastered gable wall and a prominent wooden entrance porch. The porch is constructed from vertical dark wood planks and houses a double wooden door with a decorative diamond pattern. Above the porch, a single small square window is set into the white wall. At the very peak of the steep gabled roof sits a small, simple wooden bell tower. The building is situated in a grassy clearing surrounded by dense evergreen trees under an overcast, flat sky. The style is a mid-20th-century interpretation of traditional Alpine architecture.

entities.notes: Characterized by the use of traditional materials like wood and plaster in simplified, geometric forms.
